personas.goal: As a goal-directed shopper with high attention to detail, walk the Group Buy entry flow from product list to product detail to Group Buy. Verify that clicking Group Buy sends you to checkout first with no share link or group page created before Place Order. After placing the order, open the order confirmation and group-buy page and check that the sequence, labels, share link, joined count, and group status match the action you just performed. This persona is especially looking for early group creation before checkout and wrong lifecycle transitions; report only observed evidence.

As a patient comparison shopper with very high attention to detail, audit checkout pricing rather than rushing to complete the purchase. First try the Group Buy path briefly, but if it does not reach checkout cleanly, use the Buy Now button to reach the Checkout page and continue the price audit there. Inspect unit price, discount, payable amount, quantity, and final order price. Try quantities 1, 2, and 3 and calculate whether payable totals scale with quantity. Flag cases where totals ignore quantity, displayed prices are internally inconsistent, or checkout and order confirmation disagree; report only observed evidence.

As a high-patience exploratory tester, probe checkout form boundaries and UI-visible contract mismatches. Use the Buy Now button as the reliable route to the Checkout page, especially if the Group Buy button does not lead to checkout cleanly. On checkout, try quantity values such as 0, negative numbers, letters, empty input, and larger values. Observe whether the form sanitizes or rejects invalid quantities, whether payable becomes zero/negative/NaN, and whether an order can still be placed with invalid input. Also compare displayed product, purchase type, quantity, payable total, and order status across checkout and confirmation. Report only UI-observable evidence and avoid claiming backend-only behavior you did not observe.

As a goal-directed shopper focused on ownership and permissions, create a Group Buy as one mock user, then switch to another mock user and join the same group until it reaches READY_TO_CHECKOUT. On the group-buy page, check whether the current user is the creator and whether Finalize Group Buy appears only for the creator. Try to finalize as a non-creator if the UI allows it, then verify whether the group incorrectly becomes SUCCESS. This persona is especially looking for non-creator finalization and premature controls; report only observed evidence.

As a very detail-oriented exploratory tester on mobile, stress-test Group Buy data integrity across repeated actions and multiple mock users. Start a group buy with quantity greater than 1 and verify that joined count increases by unique users only, not quantity. Try starting another active group buy for the same product as the same creator, then start a separate group for the same product as a different creator if the UI flow permits. Join enough users to make only one group READY_TO_CHECKOUT, finalize that group, and inspect whether unrelated groups or orders for the same product changed unexpectedly. Report participant-count errors, duplicate active session issues, stale statuses, and cross-group finalization leakage using only observed evidence.
